master_prompt:
turn this proof asset into a short instagram reel script.

rules:
- speak in third-person brand voice, not in the member's voice
- do not pretend to be the client or invent diary details
- keep the script concrete, grounded, and low-hype
- if the asset is stats-led, default to music-only and text motion instead of narration
- if the asset is testimonial, story strip, or partner split, keep it to 2-4 short sentences
- sentence 1 should name the visible result or contrast
- sentence 2 should explain the behavioral shift or why it matters
- optional sentence 3 can anchor it as real member progress from torch method
- never use character acting, fake quotes, or claims that are not visible in the asset

Testimonial.goal: compress one visible result into a short trust hit that sounds calm and credible.
Testimonial.voice_posture: steady, grounded, lightly warm, not salesy
structure: explain why that result matters in real behavior
close with a brief proof anchor
template: the useful part is the shift underneath it: [behavioral change].
design_notes: these should feel like distilled proof, not testimonials performed by an actor.
the first line needs to track what is plainly visible in the art.
avoid overclaiming the life change if the image only shows one slice of progress.
Testimonial.shell: [headline result] the useful part is the shift underneath it: [behavioral change]. real torch method member progress.

sample_items.title: From isolation to confidence.
sample_items.sample_script: From isolation to confidence. the useful part is not just the headline. it is the steadier behavior sitting underneath that result. real torch method member progress.
sample_items.design_rationale: the visual is a result headline, so the audio should explain the behavioral meaning without overselling.

sample_items.title: Dating feels navigable now.
sample_items.sample_script: Dating feels navigable now. the useful part is not just the headline. it is the steadier behavior sitting underneath that result. real torch method member progress.
sample_items.design_rationale: the visual is a result headline, so the audio should explain the behavioral meaning without overselling.

sample_items.title: Clear guidance beats vague motivation.
sample_items.sample_script: Clear guidance beats vague motivation. the useful part is not just the headline. it is the steadier behavior sitting underneath that result. real torch method member progress.
sample_items.design_rationale: the visual is a result headline, so the audio should explain the behavioral meaning without overselling.

Story Strip.goal: use the implied before-and-after sequence to make progress feel observable.
Story Strip.voice_posture: calm, observant, slightly more narrative, still restrained
structure: explain why that change is meaningful
close with a brief proof anchor
template: that is what real progress looks like when it starts showing up in actual situations.
design_notes: this family can carry slightly more narration because the image already implies sequence.
keep the story at the pattern level unless the panel text itself gives concrete detail.
the value is observability, not melodrama.
Story Strip.shell: [name]'s shift here is simple: [before-state]. instead, the move became [better behavior]. that is what real progress looks like when it starts showing up in actual situations. real torch method member progress.

sample_items.title: Story Strip / Matt
sample_items.sample_script: Matt's shift here is simple: less guessing, more grounded follow-through. that is what progress looks like when it starts showing up in actual situations. real torch method member progress.
sample_items.design_rationale: the visual already implies sequence, so the audio should simply guide the viewer through the shift.

sample_items.title: Story Strip / Ethan
sample_items.sample_script: Ethan's shift here is simple: less guessing, more grounded follow-through. that is what progress looks like when it starts showing up in actual situations. real torch method member progress.
sample_items.design_rationale: the visual already implies sequence, so the audio should simply guide the viewer through the shift.

Partner Split.goal: highlight the contrast between old pattern and better pattern without sounding preachy.
Partner Split.voice_posture: clear, direct, slightly firmer than testimonial
structure: say why the shift lands better
template: the contrast here is the point: [old pattern] versus [new pattern].
[new pattern] tends to land better because it reads as calmer and clearer.
design_notes: these are contrast reels, so the script should do less work than the image.
the voice should sharpen the difference, not restate every label on screen.
keep the explanation practical, not moralistic.
avoid: loading it with too many adjectives
making it sound like a lecture
Partner Split.shell: the contrast here is the point: [old pattern] versus [new pattern]. [new pattern] tends to land better because it reads as calmer and clearer. real torch method member progress.

sample_items.title: Partner Split / Charlie
sample_items.sample_script: the contrast here is the point: less second-guessing, more calm directness. that kind of shift tends to land better because it reads clearer and steadier. real torch method member progress.
sample_items.design_rationale: the visual is a contrast machine, so the audio should sharpen the contrast and get out of the way.

sample_items.title: Partner Split / Matt
sample_items.sample_script: the contrast here is the point: less second-guessing, more calm directness. that kind of shift tends to land better because it reads clearer and steadier. real torch method member progress.
sample_items.design_rationale: the visual is a contrast machine, so the audio should sharpen the contrast and get out of the way.

Stats Grid.goal: let the numbers and layout do most of the persuasion.
design_notes: this family is usually stronger with music, movement, and readable text.
narration can easily clutter a stats asset and slow down comprehension.
only add voice if the headline needs a single clean emphasis line.

sample_items.title: Real proof beats empty hype.
sample_items.sample_script: optional one-line cap only: Real proof beats empty hype.
sample_items.design_rationale: the visual is already dense with proof, so audio should be optional and minimal.

sample_items.title: The system works because men actually finish it.
sample_items.sample_script: optional one-line cap only: The system works because men actually finish it.
sample_items.design_rationale: the visual is already dense with proof, so audio should be optional and minimal.

Stats.goal: deliver a fast credibility pulse without slowing the viewer down.
design_notes: treat these as proof punctuation marks in the feed.
the best outcome is immediate legibility, not richer storytelling.
if we add voice, it should feel like a caption read, not a monologue.

sample_items.title: Real proof beats empty hype.
sample_items.sample_script: optional one-line cap only: Real proof beats empty hype.
sample_items.design_rationale: the visual is already dense with proof, so audio should be optional and minimal.
